title: Atractocarpus confertus
taxonomy: Atractocarpus confertus is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder or coffee family. This family includes around 13,000 species and is one of the largest families of flowering plants. Atractocarpus confertus is further classified in the genus Atractocarpus, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia and surrounding regions. The species Atractocarpus confertus is found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Australia, including Queensland and New South Wales. Atractocarpus confertus is a member of the family Rubiaceae and the genus Atractocarpus, with its specific classification being Atractocarpus confertus.
